item.title: Reve: Bring your ideas to life
item.description: Reve Image (Halfmoon): A model trained from the ground up to excel at prompt adherence, aesthetics, and typography. Make any image you can imagine with Reve.

item.title: Menelaos Vergis
item.description: Things I work on

item.title: The Desperation of NYTimes
item.description: I recently got suckered into subscribing to NYTimes because I wanted to read an article behind a paywall and I couldn't find an easy and quick alternative. I didn't mind the $2.00 a month. But I took offense to what happened after I paid.
item.author: (rozumem)

item.title: Do the hardest thing
item.description: Most small business ideas are a hard job attached to an easy idea. Instead, you should do your hardest idea.

item.title: Good teachers don’t cheat – jasonkena's blog
item.description: A unified view of policy gradients, self-distillation, and Pedagogical RL

item.title: France Needs 45 Years to Grow Its Way Out
item.description: Cutting spending when you already spend 57% of GDP gives you six times less growth than the same cut at 20%. France is on the wrong part of the curve.

item.title: My Agent Skill for Test-Driven Development - SaturnCI - Continuous Integration for Ruby on Rails
item.description: AI agents tend to be, at least as of this writing, lousy at writing tests. The tests they write are often vague, cryptic, overcomplicated, hacky, disorganized, ...

item.title: The tiniest logging stack: Fluent Bit, Parquet and DuckDB
item.description: A minimal architecture for shipping logs to S3 as Parquet, compacting small files, and querying them efficiently with DuckDB.
item.author: (David Guerrero)

item.title: I deleted WebSockets and haven’t looked back
item.description: Why I swapped Turbo Stream broadcasts for polling after a paying customer got stuck with an infinite spinner.

item.title: "maybe later" was a feature - arnorhs.dev
item.description: The most valueble code in my repo, is the code I didn't write.

item.title: Inside FAISS: Billion-Scale Similarity Search
item.description: How Facebook AI Research solved the problem of finding similarity at billion-scale. An interactive exploration of IVF, HNSW, and Product Quantization.
item.author: (Tom Salembien)

item.title: VoidZero is joining Cloudflare
item.description: VoidZero, the team behind Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, Oxc, and Vite+, is joining Cloudflare. Vite stays open source, vendor-agnostic, and built for everyone.

item.title: Military experts or arms industry insiders? UK media fails to disclose defence sector links in nearly 60% of cases - AOAV
item.description: Here AOAV finds that UK media outlets frequently present retired senior military figures as independent defence experts without disclosing their commercial ties to defence, security, intelligence, and technology industries, potentially obscuring conflicts of interest in public debates over war and military spending.

item.title: fopen is Magic! - Find Out What You've Been Missing All These Years!
item.description: Dave explains the magic of fopen and the multiple ways it can be used that many programmers do not even know about.
item.author: (Dave's Garage)

item.title: Max Leiter
item.description: with apologies to Terry Bisson

item.title: Anti-AI nostalgia and the cult of the past
item.description: Programmers were better back in the day, weren’t they? Back when we had real programmers. Not just people who got paid to write code, but people who lived it, who were obsessed with their craft, and whose code was a lively expression of themselves. Hackers were hackers in those days before money took over the industry.

item.title: Discovering the LLM's curious and remarkable world knowledge of open data on the web.
item.description: Turns out you can explore data with AI even if you don't have any... data.

item.title: My son, the OpenAI server - byandrev
item.description: Sam Altman has said that he finds the comparison made regarding the energy consumption of artificial intelligence unfair, and that no one questions how much energy is used to "train" a human.

item.title: The True Cost of the DOM
item.description: Part 5 of 8 — Series index

item.title: Evals or You're Blind — Pydantic AI × Pydantic Evals × crabbox
item.description: A runnable demo: realistic Pydantic Evals grading a Pydantic AI agent, executed on disposable boxes via crabbox. Built around the 7 anti-lessons from PyCon DE.

item.title: How to Just Do a Thing
item.description: I keep reading books from the 19th century and earlier, fiction and nonfiction, because I envy something about the lives of the people in them.

item.title: I Built a Local News Website
item.description: The Boise started as a personal experiment in local media, AI, maps, and UX. It became a way to rethink what local news can feel like when it is built around how people actually live.
item.author: (https://thomasmcgee.co)

item.title: Structure of Every LLM Chat
item.description: Role tagging is not cosmetic. It shapes how the model responds, how context is managed across multiple turns, and how application developers constrain and direct model behaviour at a structural level. Understanding this format is the difference between using an LLM and building reliably on top of one.

item.title: For EU Open Source, the Budget Is the Policy
item.description: The EU's new Open Source Strategy is a real step forward, but it still has no procurement teeth. Here is what changed since 2020, what hasn't, and why the budget Brussels already controls is the policy that matters most.

item.title: OpenAI has a branding problem
item.description: The company that led the consumer AI race might be losing the naming one.
item.author: (Jordan Fulghum)

item.title: Quill Grid
item.description: Quill Grid: The Starlink for AI becomes a network. An optional, opt-in distributed AI grid where idle Quill One devices share spare capacity, earn credits, and donate AI hours.

item.title: Quill Grid
item.description: Quill Grid: The Starlink for AI becomes a network. An optional, opt-in distributed AI grid where idle Quill One devices share spare capacity, earn credits, and donate AI hours.

item.title: You Don't Need an Outbox - EventSourcingDB
item.description: The outbox pattern has quietly become canonical in microservice tutorials. It has a name, library support, conference talks, and a steady stream of blog posts that walk through implementing it. It's blessed. And yet, every time it shows up in a system, it's a sign that something else has gone wrong upstream.

item.title: Building with Agents — Readables
item.description: Agents get you to 90% fast. The last 10% is where the engineering lives — and it still belongs to you. Notes on the last mile, decisions, and scope creep.

item.title: The Approach to Equilibrium
item.description: Coffee cools, eggs scramble, the past never comes back. Yet the laws underneath run just as well in reverse. Here is the simplest machine I know that holds both truths at once, and shows where the missing order really goes.

item.title: My Software North Star
item.description: Loris Cro's Blog

item.title: The Agentic Test Pyramid — Matthew Boston
item.description: The classic test pyramid assumes every test is deterministic. The moment a model is part of your system, that assumption breaks. You need a second axis.
item.author: (Matthew Boston)

item.title: ESP32-S31 Dual-Core RISC-V + Multi-Protocol SoC
item.description: Overview Features Products & Resources Product Selector

item.title: Server-Side WebRTC Noise Reduction with Pion, FFmpeg, and RNN Models
item.description: An engineering note on routing WebRTC Opus audio through a Go/Pion server, decoding it to PCM, filtering it with FFmpeg arnndn, and validating the tradeoffs before real-time use.

item.title: 10 million requests in my bot black hole... Here is some information: | Glade Art Blog
item.description: And so, ladies and gentlemen, we have hit the 10,000,000 serves threshold of the Data Export tar pit. This is a whole new level of bot trapping, and it's pea...
item.author: (Jackie Glade)

item.title: The Bloat: Devlog
item.description: Listening to a news broadcast is like smoking a cigarette and crushing the butt in the ashtray

item.title: Vivaldi (Chrome) ruining website colors - Yee Chie's Corner
item.description: I recently tried Vivaldi browser and noticed my website’s colors looked wrong. Vivaldi’s (or any Chrome-based browser) aggressive color management adjusts...

item.title: The case for memory safe desktop Linux distribution
item.description: I am writing this after setting up new laptop, so I have some thought about how I'd like my system to work and decided to write them down. I...

item.title: What your router knows but won't tell you - David E. Weekly
item.description: A while back my home network started acting strange. Not broken, exactly. Just a low-grade wrongness. Video calls would glitch for a few seconds and come back.
item.author: (David E. Weekly)

item.title: Opus 4.8 vs Opus 4.7 vs GPT-5.5 vs Composer 2.5 - 50 Real PRs in Go and Rust
item.description: I graded four frontier coding models on 50 real merged PRs in Go and Rust - not just whether tests pass, but craft, equivalence, and cost. Opus 4.8 led on craft in both.

item.title: An Open Source Motorized XYZ Micro-Manipulator - Affordable sub µm Motion Control
item.description:
A low-cost and easy to build 3-Axis motion plattform is presented. It can achieve step sizes down to 50nm while supporting arbitrary acceleration limited motion paths in a working volume of 23x23x23mm³. The simple G-Coder interface allows easy control from printer-hosts or custom software. The device can be used in applications, like automated microscopy, optical alignment or maskless lithography.
✅ Project on Github: https://github.com/0x23/MicroManipulatorStepper
🠲 I am looking for a better project name? Any ideas are welcome.
*** Video Chapters ***
00:00 Intro 01:18 Overview 01:52 Miniature Ball Joints 03:41 Stepper Motors 04:53 Magnetic Encoders 08:18 Electronics 10:25 Firmware 13:11 Kinematic Model 14:33 Build & Assembly 19:05 Results & Applications 24:25 Whats Next ?
item.author: (Diffraction Limited)
